title: AGENTS.md — MPCurver Package
text: MPCurver (formerly smoothEMr) implements MPCurve statistical model trajectory/pseudotime inference high-dimensional data (e.g. single-cell RNA-seq). package now two fitting families: cavi — recommended/default variational pipeline, explicit Gaussian posterior feature trajectories. csmooth_em / smooth_em — legacy MAP/EM-style algorithms retained benchmarking, backward compatibility, method comparison. user-facing workflows framed around fit_mpcurve() / do_mpcurve(), default cavi backend. Package goals: 1. Performance — vectorize hot loops, enforce sparse matrix usage, add parallelism 2. API usability — cleaner pipe-friendly interface, better defaults, unified naming 3. Documentation & tests — testthat coverage, parameter-choice vignette

title: Introduction to MPCurver
text: MPCurver fits smooth trajectory models pseudotime inference high-dimensional data. Given n×dn \times d data matrix, package recovers latent ordering nn samples fitting Gaussian mixture model whose component means constrained vary smoothly along ordered axis. main entry point fit_mpcurve(), now exposes CAVI-first public interface. Legacy smooth_em csmooth_em routines remain available lower-level functions historical comparison, part recommended wrapper workflow.

title: Introduction to MPCurver
text: observe data matrix X=(xij)∈ℝn×d. X = (x_{ij}) \\mathbb{R}^{n \times d}. MPCurve model posits: sample ii latent position ti∈{1,…,K}t_i \\{1,\dots,K\} along ordered grid KK components. feature jj smooth trajectory uj=(uj1,…,ujK)⊤u_j = (u_{j1},\dots,u_{jK})^\top across KK components. Observations follow Gaussian likelihood: xij∣ti=k,uj,σj2∼N(ujk,σj2). x_{ij} \mid t_i = k,\; u_j,\; \sigma_j^2 \;\sim\; N(u_{jk},\; \sigma_j^2). trajectory regularised Gaussian Markov random field (GMRF) prior: uj∼N(0,(λjQK)−1), u_j \sim N\!\bigl(0,\; (\lambda_j\, Q_K)^{-1}\bigr),  QKQ_K random-walk precision matrix KK grid points λj>0\lambda_j > 0 controls smoothness feature jj. σj2\sigma_j^2 λj\lambda_j feature-specific estimated data.

title: Introduction to MPCurver
text: MPCurver uses coordinate-ascent variational inference (CAVI). posterior latent positions trajectories approximated mean-field family: q(T,U)=∏=1nqi(ti)∏j=1dqj(uj), q(T,U) = \prod_{=1}^n q_i(t_i)\;\prod_{j=1}^d q_j(u_j),  qj(uj)=N(mj,Sj)q_j(u_j) = N(m_j, S_j). CAVI maximises evidence lower bound (ELBO) one block time. coordinate update closed-form solution: every update exact coordinate maximiser, recorded elbo_trace guaranteed non-decreasing. key feature CAVI updates posterior uncertainty term Sj,kkS_{j,kk}, appears position update variance update. distinguishes variational approach simpler plug-EM algorithm.

title: CAVI Initialization Diagnostics on a Harder Model-Matched Benchmark
code: make_gamma_from_z <- function(z, K) {   gamma <- matrix(0, nrow = length(z), ncol = K)   gamma[cbind(seq_along(z), z)] <- 1   gamma }  align_mpcurve_fit <- function(fit, sim) {   est_mu <- fit$fit$posterior$mean   true_mu <- sim$mu   z_hat <- max.col(fit$gamma, ties.method = "first")    score <- function(mu_est, z_est) {     c(       z_acc = mean(z_est == sim$z),       mu_cor = mean(vapply(seq_len(nrow(true_mu)), function(j) {         suppressWarnings(stats::cor(mu_est[j, ], true_mu[j, ]))       }, numeric(1)), na.rm = TRUE),       mu_rmse = sqrt(mean((mu_est - true_mu)^2))     )   }    forward <- score(est_mu, z_hat)   reverse <- score(est_mu[, ncol(est_mu):1, drop = FALSE], ncol(est_mu) + 1L - z_hat)   if (reverse["z_acc"] > forward["z_acc"]) {     list(       flip = TRUE,       metrics = reverse,       mu_hat = est_mu[, ncol(est_mu):1, drop = FALSE]     )   } else {     list(       flip = FALSE,       metrics = forward,       mu_hat = est_mu     )   } }  summarise_fit <- function(fit, sim, label) {   aligned <- align_mpcurve_fit(fit, sim)   data.frame(     fit = label,     z_accuracy = aligned$metrics["z_acc"],     mean_curve_cor = aligned$metrics["mu_cor"],     mu_rmse = aligned$metrics["mu_rmse"],     sigma_cor = suppressWarnings(stats::cor(log(fit$params$sigma2), log(sim$sigma2))),     lambda_cor = suppressWarnings(stats::cor(log(fit$fit$lambda_vec), log(sim$lambda_vec))),     min_elbo_delta = min(diff(fit$elbo_trace)),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) }  init_snapshot <- function(method) {   fit0 <- cavi(     X,     K = 16,     method = method,     rw_q = 2,     ridge = 1e-3,     discretization = "equal",     max_iter = 0,     tol = 1e-8,     verbose = FALSE   )   fit1 <- do_cavi(fit0, iter = 1L, tol = 0, verbose = FALSE)    s0 <- align_mpcurve_fit(as_mpcurve(fit0), sim)$metrics   s1 <- align_mpcurve_fit(as_mpcurve(fit1), sim)$metrics    data.frame(     method = method,     z_acc_iter0 = s0["z_acc"],     mean_curve_cor_iter0 = s0["mu_cor"],     z_acc_iter1 = s1["z_acc"],     mean_curve_cor_iter1 = s1["mu_cor"],     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) }

code: gamma_true <- make_gamma_from_z(sim$z, K = 16)  fit_equal_default <- fit_mpcurve(   X,   algorithm = "cavi",   method = "PCA",   K = 16,   rw_q = 2,   iter = 200,   ridge = 1e-3,   tol = 1e-8,   discretization = "equal",   verbose = FALSE )  fit_equal_oracle <- fit_mpcurve(   X,   algorithm = "cavi",   K = 16,   rw_q = 2,   iter = 200,   ridge = 1e-3,   tol = 1e-8,   discretization = "equal",   responsibilities_init = gamma_true,   verbose = FALSE ) equal_tbl <- rbind(   summarise_fit(fit_equal_default, sim, "default + equal"),   summarise_fit(fit_equal_oracle, sim, "oracle + equal") ) print(equal_tbl, row.names = FALSE) #>              fit z_accuracy mean_curve_cor     mu_rmse  sigma_cor lambda_cor #>  default + equal    0.76125      0.9908451 1.163495904 -0.1335716  0.9473555 #>   oracle + equal    1.00000      0.9999941 0.007487933  0.9967315  0.9659732 #>  min_elbo_delta #>    6.687940e-07 #>    3.271271e-08

code: summarise_fiedler_init <- function(X, sim, K, discretization = "quantile", k = NULL) {   init <- if (is.null(k)) {     initialize_ordering_csmooth(       X,       K = K,       method = "fiedler",       discretization = discretization,       modelName = "homoskedastic"     )   } else {     initialize_ordering_csmooth(       X,       K = K,       method = "fiedler",       discretization = discretization,       modelName = "homoskedastic",       k = k     )   }    keep_idx <- init$keep_idx %||% seq_len(nrow(X))   z_sub <- sim$z[keep_idx]   cr <- init$cluster_rank   acc_forward <- mean(cr == z_sub)   acc_reverse <- mean((K + 1L - cr) == z_sub)    data.frame(     k_label = if (is.null(k)) "default auto-k" else sprintf("fixed k=%d", k),     k_used = init$ordering$k_used,     n_components = init$ordering$n_components,     coverage = length(keep_idx) / nrow(X),     init_accuracy = max(acc_forward, acc_reverse),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) } fiedler_worked <- rbind(   summarise_fiedler_init(X, sim, K = 16, discretization = "quantile"),   summarise_fiedler_init(X, sim, K = 16, discretization = "quantile", k = 15) ) print(fiedler_worked, row.names = FALSE) #>         k_label k_used n_components coverage init_accuracy #>  default auto-k    113            1 1.000000     0.4300000 #>      fixed k=15     15           16 0.070625     0.0619469

title: Mathematical Details of the MPCurver CAVI Algorithm
text: observe matrix X=(xij)∈ℝn×d, X = (x_{ij}) \\mathbb{R}^{n \times d},  cells indexed =1,…,ni = 1,\dots,n features indexed j=1,…,dj = 1,\dots,d.

title: Mathematical Details of the MPCurver CAVI Algorithm
text: cell assigned one KK ordered mixture components: ci∈{1,…,K},Pr(ci=k)=πk,∑k=1Kπk=1. c_i \\{1,\dots,K\}, \qquad \Pr(c_i = k) = \pi_k, \qquad \sum_{k=1}^K \pi_k = 1.

text: variational objective evidence lower bound (ELBO), ELBO(q;θ)=𝔼q[logp(X,C,U∣θ)]−𝔼q[logq(C,U)]. \operatorname{ELBO}(q;\theta) = \mathbb E_q \big[\log p(X,C,U \mid \theta)\big] - \mathbb E_q \big[\log q(C,U)\big]. fixed θ\theta, lower bound marginal log-evidence logp(X∣θ)=log∫∑Cp(X,C,U∣θ)dU. \log p(X \mid \theta) = \log \int \sum_C p(X,C,U \mid \theta)\, dU.  precisely, logp(X∣θ)=ELBO(q;θ)+KL(q(C,U)∥p(C,U∣X,θ)). \log p(X \mid \theta) = \operatorname{ELBO}(q;\theta) + \mathrm{KL}\!\left( q(C,U)\,\|\,p(C,U \mid X,\theta) \right).  maximizing ELBO qq fixed hyperparameters equivalent driving variational approximation toward exact posterior. qq updated, maximize ELBO respect point-estimated block θ\theta. standard variational EM / variational empirical-Bayes viewpoint: first optimize variational approximation qq, optimize ELBO surrogate logp(X∣θ)\log p(X \mid \theta), hoping KL gap small, optimized ELBO close true marginal log-likelihood. Equivalently, define profiled variational objective F(θ):=maxqELBO(q;θ), F(\theta) := \max_q \operatorname{ELBO}(q;\theta),  F(θ)≤logp(X∣θ). F(\theta) \le \log p(X \mid \theta).  high level algorithm alternates : updating variational approximation qq improve bound, updating θ\theta increase optimized bound FF. procedure can viewed “approximately maximizing marginal log-likelihood”: optimize lower bound , rather intractable quantity directly. Substituting model terms ELBO definition gives ELBO(q;θ)=𝔼q[logp(C∣π)]+𝔼q[logp(X∣C,U,σ2)]+𝔼q[logp(U∣λ)]+H(qC)+H(qU), \operatorname{ELBO}(q;\theta) = \mathbb E_q[\log p(C \mid \pi)] + \mathbb E_q[\log p(X \mid C,U,\sigma^2)] + \mathbb E_q[\log p(U \mid \lambda)] + H(q_C) + H(q_U),  H(⋅)H(\cdot) denotes entropy. major coordinate-ascent idea : fix blocks except one, maximize ELBO respect block, cycle convergence. variational factors, generic mean-field update logqℓ⋆(ξℓ)=𝔼q−ℓ[logp(X,C,U∣θ)]+const, \log q_\ell^\star(\xi_\ell) = \mathbb E_{q_{-\ell}}[\log p(X,C,U \mid \theta)] + \text{const},  ξℓ\xi_\ell denotes argument current variational block. present model means: ξℓ=ci\xi_\ell = c_i current block categorical factor qi(ci)q_i(c_i), ξℓ=uj\xi_\ell = u_j current block Gaussian factor qj(uj)q_j(u_j). q−ℓq_{-\ell} means “variational factors except current one”. point-estimated parameter block θ\theta, maximize ELBO directly respect parameter block.

title: Mathematical Details of the MPCurver CAVI Algorithm
text: derive code formulas, define effective component sizes Nk:=∑=1nrik. N_k := \sum_{=1}^n r_{ik}. mixture-weight term 𝔼q[logp(C∣π)]=∑=1n∑k=1Kriklogπk. \mathbb E_q[\log p(C \mid \pi)] = \sum_{=1}^n \sum_{k=1}^K r_{ik}\log \pi_k. entropy categorical factors H(qC)=−∑=1n∑k=1Kriklogrik. H(q_C) = - \sum_{=1}^n \sum_{k=1}^K r_{ik}\log r_{ik}. Gaussian likelihood, 𝔼q[logp(X∣C,U,σ2)]=−12∑=1n∑k=1K∑j=1drik[log(2πσj2)+𝔼q[(xij−ujk)2]σj2]. \mathbb E_q[\log p(X \mid C,U,\sigma^2)] = -\frac12 \sum_{=1}^n\sum_{k=1}^K\sum_{j=1}^d r_{ik} \left[ \log(2\pi \sigma_j^2) + \frac{\mathbb E_q[(x_{ij} - u_{jk})^2]}{\sigma_j^2} \right]. Since qj(uj)=N(mj,Sj)q_j(u_j)=N(m_j,S_j), 𝔼q[(xij−ujk)2]=(xij−mjk)2+Sj,kk. \mathbb E_q[(x_{ij} - u_{jk})^2] = (x_{ij} - m_{jk})^2 + S_{j,kk}. likelihood contribution becomes −12∑=1n∑k=1K∑j=1drik[log(2πσj2)+(xij−mjk)2+Sj,kkσj2]. -\frac12 \sum_{=1}^n\sum_{k=1}^K\sum_{j=1}^d r_{ik} \left[ \log(2\pi \sigma_j^2) + \frac{(x_{ij} - m_{jk})^2 + S_{j,kk}}{\sigma_j^2} \right]. prior, 𝔼q[logp(U∣λ)]=12∑j=1d[rQlogλj+log|QK|+−λj𝔼q(uj⊤QKuj)]. \mathbb E_q[\log p(U \mid \lambda)] = \frac12 \sum_{j=1}^d \left[ r_Q \log \lambda_j + \log |Q_K|_+ - \lambda_j \mathbb E_q(u_j^\top Q_K u_j) \right]. Using Gaussian second moments, 𝔼q(uj⊤QKuj)=mj⊤QKmj+tr(QKSj). \mathbb E_q(u_j^\top Q_K u_j) = m_j^\top Q_K m_j + \operatorname{tr}(Q_K S_j). Finally, entropy qj(uj)=N(mj,Sj)q_j(u_j)=N(m_j,S_j) H(qj)=12[K(1+log2π)+log|Sj|], H(q_j) = \frac12 \left[K(1+\log 2\pi) + \log |S_j|\right],  H(qU)=12∑j=1d[K(1+log2π)+log|Sj|]. H(q_U) = \frac12 \sum_{j=1}^d \left[K(1+\log 2\pi) + \log |S_j|\right]. Putting everything together, ELBO(q;θ)=∑=1n∑k=1Kriklogπk−∑=1n∑k=1Kriklogrik \operatorname{ELBO}(q;\theta) = \sum_{=1}^n \sum_{k=1}^K r_{ik}\log \pi_k - \sum_{=1}^n \sum_{k=1}^K r_{ik}\log r_{ik} −12∑=1n∑k=1K∑j=1drik[log(2πσj2)+(xij−mjk)2+Sj,kkσj2] -\frac12 \sum_{=1}^n\sum_{k=1}^K\sum_{j=1}^d r_{ik} \left[ \log(2\pi \sigma_j^2) + \frac{(x_{ij} - m_{jk})^2 + S_{j,kk}}{\sigma_j^2} \right] +12∑j=1d[rQlogλj+log|QK|+−λj(mj⊤QKmj+tr(QKSj))+log|Sj|+K(1+log2π)]. + \frac12 \sum_{j=1}^d \left[ r_Q \log \lambda_j + \log |Q_K|_+ - \lambda_j\left(m_j^\top Q_K m_j + \operatorname{tr}(Q_K S_j)\right) + \log |S_j| + K(1+\log 2\pi) \right]. expanded form starting point every concrete update code.

title: Model-Matched Sanity Check for CAVI MPCurver
code: make_gamma_from_z <- function(z, K) {   gamma <- matrix(0, nrow = length(z), ncol = K)   gamma[cbind(seq_along(z), z)] <- 1   gamma }  align_cavi_fit <- function(fit, sim) {   est_mu <- fit$fit$posterior$mean   true_mu <- sim$mu   z_hat <- max.col(fit$gamma, ties.method = "first")    score_orientation <- function(mu_est, z_est) {     list(       z_acc = mean(z_est == sim$z),       mu_rmse = sqrt(mean((mu_est - true_mu)^2)),       mu_cor = mean(vapply(seq_len(nrow(true_mu)), function(j) {         suppressWarnings(stats::cor(mu_est[j, ], true_mu[j, ]))       }, numeric(1)), na.rm = TRUE)     )   }    forward <- score_orientation(est_mu, z_hat)   reverse <- score_orientation(     est_mu[, ncol(est_mu):1, drop = FALSE],     ncol(est_mu) + 1L - z_hat   )    if (reverse$z_acc > forward$z_acc) {     list(       flip = TRUE,       z_hat = ncol(est_mu) + 1L - z_hat,       mu_hat = est_mu[, ncol(est_mu):1, drop = FALSE],       pi_hat = rev(fit$params$pi),       metrics = reverse     )   } else {     list(       flip = FALSE,       z_hat = z_hat,       mu_hat = est_mu,       pi_hat = fit$params$pi,       metrics = forward     )   } }  summarise_fit <- function(fit, sim, label) {   aligned <- align_cavi_fit(fit, sim)   data.frame(     fit = label,     z_accuracy = aligned$metrics$z_acc,     mean_curve_cor = aligned$metrics$mu_cor,     mu_rmse = aligned$metrics$mu_rmse,     sigma_cor = suppressWarnings(stats::cor(log(fit$params$sigma2), log(sim$sigma2))),     lambda_cor = suppressWarnings(stats::cor(log(fit$fit$lambda_vec), log(sim$lambda_vec))),     sigma_rmse = sqrt(mean((log(fit$params$sigma2) - log(sim$sigma2))^2)),     lambda_rmse = sqrt(mean((log(fit$fit$lambda_vec) - log(sim$lambda_vec))^2)),     min_elbo_delta = min(diff(fit$elbo_trace)),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) }

title: Model-Matched Sanity Check for CAVI MPCurver

Importantly, second fit uses true ordering initialization; fix parameters optimization. model-matched benchmark, main signal already visible: ordering recovery essentially perfect, posterior mean trajectories almost identical truth, sigma_j^2 recovery strong, lambda_j recovery noticeably noisier still tracks truth well log scale, supplying true ordering materially change final fit. last point useful: easy regime, main challenge initialization.
code: gamma_true <- make_gamma_from_z(sim$z, K = 12)  fit_default <- fit_mpcurve(   X,   algorithm = "cavi",   method = "PCA",   K = 12,   rw_q = 2,   iter = 150,   ridge = 1e-3,   tol = 1e-8,   verbose = FALSE )  fit_oracle <- fit_mpcurve(   X,   algorithm = "cavi",   K = 12,   rw_q = 2,   iter = 150,   ridge = 1e-3,   tol = 1e-8,   responsibilities_init = gamma_true,   verbose = FALSE ) worked_tbl <- rbind(   summarise_fit(fit_default, sim, "default init"),   summarise_fit(fit_oracle, sim, "true ordering init") ) print(worked_tbl, row.names = FALSE) #>                 fit z_accuracy mean_curve_cor     mu_rmse sigma_cor lambda_cor #>        default init          1       0.999998 0.006172715 0.9956010    0.95089 #>  true ordering init          1       0.999998 0.006172715 0.9956011    0.95089 #>  sigma_rmse lambda_rmse min_elbo_delta #>  0.05139551   0.5217530   3.513854e-04 #>  0.05139538   0.5217529   1.330045e-08

title: Conceptual and Mathematical Comparison of CAVI and csmooth_em
text: cavi, central object variational lower bound ELBO(q;θ)=𝔼q[logp(X,C,U∣θ)]−𝔼q[logq(C,U)]. \operatorname{ELBO}(q;\theta) = \mathbb E_q[\log p(X,C,U \mid \theta)] - \mathbb E_q[\log q(C,U)]. fixed θ\theta, lower bound marginal log-evidence logp(X∣θ). \log p(X \mid \theta).  Indeed, logp(X∣θ)=ELBO(q;θ)+KL(q(C,U)∥p(C,U∣X,θ)). \log p(X \mid \theta) = \operatorname{ELBO}(q;\theta) + \mathrm{KL}\!\left( q(C,U)\,\|\,p(C,U \mid X,\theta) \right). cavi can understood variational empirical Bayes: update variational approximation qq, optimize bound respect θ\theta. define profiled variational objective F(θ):=maxqELBO(q;θ), F(\theta) := \max_q \operatorname{ELBO}(q;\theta),  algorithm effectively trying increase FF, still lower bound true marginal log-likelihood.

title: Conceptual and Mathematical Comparison of CAVI and csmooth_em
text: λ\lambda fixed, csmooth_em behaves like penalized EM / MAP algorithm. representative objective JMAP(π,U,σ2;λ)=logp(X∣π,U,σ2)−12∑j=1dλjuj⊤QKuj+const, J_{\mathrm{MAP}}(\pi,U,\sigma^2;\lambda) = \log p(X \mid \pi,U,\sigma^2) - \frac12 \sum_{j=1}^d \lambda_j u_j^\top Q_K u_j + \text{const},  equivalently EM lower bound UU optimized point estimate. regime, algorithm close standard penalized EM: update responsibilities, update (π,U,σ2)(\pi,U,\sigma^2).

Instead tracks collapsed objective form Jcoll=penELBO+const−12log|H|, J_{\mathrm{coll}} = \mathrm{penELBO} + \mathrm{const} - \frac12 \log |H|,  HH local Hessian around MAP solution trajectory means. Conceptually, trying move closer marginal-likelihood objective integrating locally collapsing trajectory block UU, object explicit cavi ELBO. two algorithms genuinely optimizing different things: cavi: explicit variational lower bound model latent CC UU, csmooth_em: plug-MAP/EM objective, adaptive-ML mode collapsed / Laplace-style objective point-estimated trajectory model.

title: Conceptual and Mathematical Comparison of CAVI and csmooth_em
text: plug-responsibility update form γik∝πkexp[−12∑j=1d(log(2πσj2)+(xij−ûjk)2σj2)]. \gamma_{ik} \propto \pi_k \exp\!\left[ -\frac12 \sum_{j=1}^d \left( \log(2\pi \sigma_j^2) + \frac{(x_{ij} - \hat u_{jk})^2}{\sigma_j^2} \right) \right].

title: Conceptual and Mathematical Comparison of CAVI and csmooth_em
text: variational update q(ci)q(c_i) rik∝πkexp[−12∑j=1d(log(2πσj2)+(xij−mjk)2+Sj,kkσj2)]. r_{ik} \propto \pi_k \exp\!\left[ -\frac12 \sum_{j=1}^d \left( \log(2\pi \sigma_j^2) + \frac{(x_{ij} - m_{jk})^2 + S_{j,kk}}{\sigma_j^2} \right) \right]. extra term Sj,kkS_{j,kk} posterior uncertainty correction. immediately explains one visible empirical difference: early optimization, cavi can move aggressively treating current trajectory means certainty.

title: Conceptual and Mathematical Comparison of CAVI and csmooth_em
text: update full Gaussian variational block: qj(uj)=N(mj,Sj), q_j(u_j) = N(m_j,S_j),  Sj−1=λjQK+diag(N1/σj2,…,NK/σj2),mj=Sjbj. S_j^{-1} = \lambda_j Q_K + \operatorname{diag}(N_1/\sigma_j^2,\dots,N_K/\sigma_j^2), \qquad m_j = S_j b_j.

title: Conceptual and Mathematical Comparison of CAVI and csmooth_em
text: update penalized least-squares / MAP step: ûj=argminuj{12σj2∑=1n∑k=1Kγik(xij−ujk)2+λj2uj⊤QKuj}. \hat u_j = \arg\min_{u_j} \left\{ \frac{1}{2\sigma_j^2}\sum_{=1}^n\sum_{k=1}^K \gamma_{ik}(x_{ij}-u_{jk})^2 + \frac{\lambda_j}{2} u_j^\top Q_K u_j \right\}. one can think csmooth_em update mode corresponding Gaussian block, whereas cavi keeps mode covariance.

title: Conceptual and Mathematical Comparison of CAVI and csmooth_em
text: updates σj2\sigma_j^2 λj\lambda_j derived ELBO use posterior second moments: σj2=1n∑=1n∑k=1Krik[(xij−mjk)2+Sj,kk], \sigma_j^2 = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{=1}^n\sum_{k=1}^K r_{ik}\Big[(x_{ij}-m_{jk})^2 + S_{j,kk}\Big], λj=rank(QK)mj⊤QKmj+tr(QKSj). \lambda_j = \frac{\operatorname{rank}(Q_K)} {m_j^\top Q_K m_j + \operatorname{tr}(Q_K S_j)}.

suppressWarnings(cor(t_hat_reverse, sim$t, method = "spearman"))   reverse <- abs(rho_reverse) > abs(rho_forward)    t_hat <- if (reverse) t_hat_reverse else t_hat_forward   rho_t <- if (reverse) code: plot_primary_trace <- function(fits, algo_label) {   fit_final <- fits[["final"]]   trace <- if (fit_final$algorithm == "cavi") {     fit_final$elbo_trace   } else {     fit_final$fit$ml_trace   }    ylab <- if (fit_final$algorithm == "cavi") "ELBO" else "Collapsed ML"   x <- seq_along(trace) - 1L    plot(x, trace, type = "l", lwd = 2,        xlab = "Iteration", ylab = ylab,        main = algo_label)    stage_iters <- vapply(c("1 iter", "10 iter", "final"), function(stage) fits[[stage]]$iter, integer(1))   abline(v = stage_iters, col = c("#d9534f", "#f0ad4e", "#2b6cb0"), lty = c(3, 3, 2))   legend("bottomright",          legend = sprintf("%s (%d)", c("1 iter", "10 iter", "final"), stage_iters),          col = c("#d9534f", "#f0ad4e", "#2b6cb0"),          lty = c(3, 3, 2),          bty = "n", cex = 0.8) }  op <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) plot_primary_trace(snapshots$cavi, "CAVI") plot_primary_trace(snapshots$csmooth_em, "csmooth_em") par(op)

title: Getting Started with MPCurver CAVI
text: observe data matrix X=(xij)∈ℝn×d, X = (x_{ij}) \\mathbb{R}^{n \times d},  row ii one cell column jj one feature. single-ordering MPCurve model assumes: cell latent position ci∈{1,…,K}, c_i \\{1,\dots,K\}, feature smooth trajectory KK ordered components, written uj=(uj1,…,ujK)⊤∈ℝK, u_j = (u_{j1},\dots,u_{jK})^\top \\mathbb{R}^K, observations follow Gaussian likelihood xij∣ci=k,uj,σj2∼N(ujk,σj2), x_{ij} \mid c_i = k, u_j, \sigma_j^2 \sim N(u_{jk}, \sigma_j^2), trajectory regularized Gaussian Markov random field prior along component axis: uj∼N(0,(λjQK)−1), u_j \sim N\!\left(0, (\lambda_j Q_K)^{-1}\right),  QKQ_K random-walk precision matrix KK bins. current cavi implementation: sigma_j^2 feature-specific, lambda_j feature-specific, covariance model homoskedastic across components, posterior approximated mean-field family q(C,U)=∏=1nqi(ci)∏j=1dqj(uj). q(C,U) = \prod_{=1}^n q_i(c_i)\prod_{j=1}^d q_j(u_j).

title: Getting Started with MPCurver CAVI
text: cavi() routine performs coordinate-ascent variational inference explicit ELBO. Write rik:=qi(ci=k),Nk:=∑=1nrik, r_{ik} := q_i(c_i = k), \qquad N_k := \sum_{=1}^n r_{ik},  feature jj, qj(uj)=N(mj,Sj). q_j(u_j) = N(m_j, S_j). mean-field family q(C,U)=∏=1nqi(ci)∏j=1dqj(uj), q(C,U)=\prod_{=1}^n q_i(c_i)\prod_{j=1}^d q_j(u_j),  ELBO can written, additive constants, ℒ(q)=∑=1n∑k=1Kriklogπk−∑=1n∑k=1Kriklogrik \mathcal L(q)  = \sum_{=1}^n\sum_{k=1}^K r_{ik}\log \pi_k - \sum_{=1}^n\sum_{k=1}^K r_{ik}\log r_{ik} −12∑=1n∑k=1K∑j=1drik[log(2πσj2)+(xij−mjk)2+Sj,kkσj2] - \frac12 \sum_{=1}^n\sum_{k=1}^K\sum_{j=1}^d r_{ik} \left[ \log(2\pi \sigma_j^2) + \frac{(x_{ij}-m_{jk})^2 + S_{j,kk}}{\sigma_j^2} \right] +12∑j=1d[rank(QK)logλj−λj(mj⊤QKmj+tr(QKSj))+log|Sj|]. + \frac12 \sum_{j=1}^d \left[ \operatorname{rank}(Q_K)\log \lambda_j - \lambda_j\Big(m_j^\top Q_K m_j + \operatorname{tr}(Q_K S_j)\Big) + \log |S_j| \right]. quantity recorded elbo_trace. updates used cavi() obtained maximizing ELBO one block time.

title: Getting Started with MPCurver CAVI
text: Fix rikr_{ik}, σj2\sigma_j^2, λj\lambda_j. ELBO terms involving uju_j quadratic, optimal variational factor Gaussian: logqj⋆(uj)=−12uj⊤Ajuj+uj⊤bj+const, \log q_j^\star(u_j) = -\frac12 u_j^\top A_j u_j + u_j^\top b_j + \text{const},  Aj=λjQK+diag(N1/σj2,…,NK/σj2), A_j = \lambda_j Q_K + \operatorname{diag}\!\left(N_1/\sigma_j^2,\dots,N_K/\sigma_j^2\right),  bjk=1σj2∑=1nrikxij. b_{jk} = \frac{1}{\sigma_j^2}\sum_{=1}^n r_{ik} x_{ij}.  Therefore, Sj=Aj−1,mj=Sjbj. S_j = A_j^{-1}, \qquad m_j = S_j b_j. exactly linear-algebra step implemented cavi().

title: Getting Started with MPCurver CAVI
text: Fix q(U)q(U), π\pi, σj2\sigma_j^2. Collecting ELBO terms involving row ri⋅r_{\cdot} gives logrik∝logπk−12∑j=1d[log(2πσj2)+(xij−mjk)2+Sj,kkσj2]. \log r_{ik} \propto \log \pi_k - \frac12 \sum_{j=1}^d \left[ \log(2\pi \sigma_j^2) + \frac{(x_{ij}-m_{jk})^2 + S_{j,kk}}{\sigma_j^2} \right].  cell-position posterior updated row-wise softmax: ri⋅←softmax(ℓi1,…,ℓiK), r_{\cdot} \leftarrow \operatorname{softmax}\!\big(\ell_{i1},\dots,\ell_{iK}\big),  ℓik\ell_{ik} equal right-hand side . extra Sj,kkS_{j,kk} term uncertainty correction distinguishes update pure plug-EM step.

title: Getting Started with MPCurver CAVI
text: Fixing everything else, maximize ∑k=1KNklogπk \sum_{k=1}^K N_k \log \pi_k  subject ∑kπk=1\sum_k \pi_k = 1. Lagrange multiplier gives πk=Nkn. \pi_k = \frac{N_k}{n}.

title: Getting Started with MPCurver CAVI
text: Fixing q(U)q(U) q(C)q(C), differentiate ELBO respect σj2\sigma_j^2. stationary point σj2=1n∑=1n∑k=1Krik[(xij−mjk)2+Sj,kk]. \sigma_j^2 = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{=1}^n \sum_{k=1}^K r_{ik}\Big[(x_{ij}-m_{jk})^2 + S_{j,kk}\Big]. feature variance updated posterior expected residual sum squares.

title: Getting Started with MPCurver CAVI
text: Fixing q(U)q(U), prior part ELBO depends λj\lambda_j: 12rank(QK)logλj−12λj(mj⊤QKmj+tr(QKSj)). \frac12 \operatorname{rank}(Q_K)\log \lambda_j - \frac12 \lambda_j \Big(m_j^\top Q_K m_j + \operatorname{tr}(Q_K S_j)\Big).  Setting derivative zero yields λj=rank(QK)mj⊤QKmj+tr(QKSj). \lambda_j = \frac{\operatorname{rank}(Q_K)} {m_j^\top Q_K m_j + \operatorname{tr}(Q_K S_j)}.

title: Feature Partitioning with MPCurver
text: single-ordering model vignette("mpcurve_intro") assumes features share one latent sample ordering. many applications, however, different groups features may reflect different underlying processes, ordering. partition model MM orderings introduces: feature-level assignment zj∈{1,…,M}z_j \\{1,\dots,M\}, MM independent latent orderings ti(m)∈{1,…,K}t_i^{(m)} \\{1,\dots,K\}, m=1,…,Mm = 1,\dots,M, MM sets smooth trajectories uj(m)∈ℝKu_j^{(m)} \\mathbb{R}^K. observation model becomes: xij∣zj=m,ti(m)=k∼N(ujk(m),σj2). x_{ij} \mid z_j = m,\; t_i^{(m)} = k \;\sim\; N\!\bigl(u_{jk}^{(m)},\; \sigma_j^2\bigr). trajectory retains GMRF prior: uj(m)∼N(0,(λj(m)QK)−1). u_j^{(m)} \sim N\!\bigl(0,\; (\lambda_j^{(m)} Q_K)^{-1}\bigr). goal recover partition (features belong ordering) orderings . generalises intrinsic_dim concept vignette("mpcurve_intro"): model MM orderings intrinsic dimension MM. standard single-ordering fit special case M=1M = 1.

title: Feature Partitioning with MPCurver
text: MPCurver uses soft partition approach: rather assigning feature exactly one ordering, maintains probabilistic weights wjm∈[0,1]w_{jm} \[0,1] (∑mwjm=1\sum_m w_{jm} = 1) describing much feature jj belongs ordering mm.

title: Feature Partitioning with MPCurver
text: feature jj ordering mm, assignment update uses expected log-likelihood score ℓjmlike=𝔼q(C(m))q(uj(m))[logp(x⋅j∣zj=m,C(m),uj(m))], \ell_{jm}^{\mathrm{like}} = \mathbb{E}_{q(C^{(m)}) q(u_j^{(m)})} \big[ \log p(x_{\cdot j} \mid z_j = m,\; C^{(m)},\; u_j^{(m)}) \big],  expands ℓjmlike=∑,krik(m)[−12log(2πσj2)−(xij−mjk(m))2+Sj,kk(m)2σj2]. \ell_{jm}^{\mathrm{like}} = \sum_{,k} r_{ik}^{(m)}   \left[     -\tfrac{1}{2}\log(2\pi\sigma_j^2)     - \frac{(x_{ij} - m_{jk}^{(m)})^2 + S_{j,kk}^{(m)}}{2\,\sigma_j^2}   \right].

title: Feature Partitioning with MPCurver
text: default fixed-uniform assignment prior, feature weights updated via softmax temperature TT: wjm∝exp(ℓjmlike/T). w_{jm} \;\propto\; \exp\!\bigl(\ell_{jm}^{\mathrm{like}} / T\bigr). assignment_prior = "dirichlet", score augmented current variational expectation ordering-usage weights: $$ w_{jm} \;\propto\; \exp\!\Bigl( \bigl[\ell_{jm}^{\mathrm{like}} + \mathbb{E}_q \log \omega_m\bigr] / T \Bigr), $$ q(ω)=Dirichlet(α+∑jwj1,…,α+∑jwjM). q(\omega) = \operatorname{Dirichlet}\!\left( \alpha + \sum_j w_{j1},\dots,\alpha + \sum_j w_{jM} \right). CAVI ordering update uses feature-weighted responsibilities: contribution feature jj ordering mm scaled wjmw_{jm}. annealing schedule starts T=TstartT = T_\text{start} decreases geometrically T=TendT = T_\text{end}. high temperature, features contribute evenly across orderings; T→1T \1, weights sharpen toward hard assignments.

title: Feature Partitioning with MPCurver
text: default fixed-uniform prior, partition objective ℒpartition=−dlogM+TH(w)+∑m=1M[𝔼qlogp(C(m)∣π(m))+H(q(C(m)))]+∑j∑m=1M[wjmℓjmlike−KL(q(uj(m))∥p(uj(m)))]. \mathcal{L}_\text{partition} = - d \log M   + T\,H(w)   + \sum_{m=1}^M     \Big[       \mathbb{E}_q \log p(C^{(m)} \mid \pi^{(m)})       + H(q(C^{(m)}))     \Big]   + \sum_j \sum_{m=1}^M     \Big[       w_{jm}\,\ell_{jm}^{\mathrm{like}}       - \mathrm{KL}\!\bigl(q(u_j^{(m)}) \,\|\, p(u_j^{(m)})\bigr)     \Big]. assignment_prior = "dirichlet", fixed term -d log M + T H(w) replaced full variational assignment block ∑j,mwjm𝔼qlogωm+TH(w)−KL(q(ω)∥p(ω)). \sum_{j,m} w_{jm}\,\mathbb{E}_q \log \omega_m + T\,H(w) - \mathrm{KL}\!\bigl(q(\omega) \,\|\, p(\omega)\bigr). cases, lambda_sd_prior_rate > 0, recorded objective also includes induced prior contribution ∑j,mlogp(λj(m)),logp(λ)=log(ρ/2)−32logλ−ρλ−1/2, \sum_{j,m} \log p(\lambda_j^{(m)}), \qquad \log p(\lambda) = \log(\rho/2) - \tfrac{3}{2}\log \lambda - \rho \lambda^{-1/2},  stored objective_history remains aligned actual penalized updates.

title: Feature Partitioning with MPCurver
text: Dimension selection now unified soft-CAVI framework fit_mpcurve(greedy = ...). greedy = "forward" treats intrinsic_dim upper bound compares M versus M + 1 sequentially starting M = 1. greedy = "backward" first fits upper bound, removes one active ordering time compares M versus M - 1. greedy modes always compare models using fixed-requested-M comparison objective, internally run drop_unused_ordering = FALSE even ask compact final returned view. True vs inferred feature partitions.
code: fit_forward <- fit_mpcurve(   X,   intrinsic_dim = 4,   greedy = "forward",   K = 16,   drop_unused_ordering = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )  fit_backward <- fit_mpcurve(   X,   intrinsic_dim = 4,   greedy = "backward",   K = 16,   drop_unused_ordering = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE ) summary_tbl <- data.frame(   fit = c("soft (fixed M=2)", "greedy forward", "greedy backward"),   displayed_dim = c(     fit_partition$intrinsic_dim,     fit_forward$intrinsic_dim,     fit_backward$intrinsic_dim   ),   selected_dim = c(     fit_partition$requested_intrinsic_dim,     fit_forward$greedy_selection$selected_intrinsic_dim,     fit_backward$greedy_selection$selected_intrinsic_dim   ) ) print(summary_tbl, row.names = FALSE) #>               fit displayed_dim selected_dim #>  soft (fixed M=2)             2            2 #>    greedy forward             2            2 #>   greedy backward             2            2 assignment_heatmap(   truth    = truth,   soft     = fit_partition$partition$assign,   forward  = fit_forward$partition$assign,   backward = fit_backward$partition$assign,   main = "Feature partition comparison" )

Greedy search always compares models fixed-M objective. Start intrinsic_dim = 2. partition unclear, consider M=3M = 3 allow third ordering capture residual structure. Specify independent init methods. method = c("fiedler", "PCA") uses spectral ordering first axis PC1 second explicit PCA-based ordering. want later PCA axes explicit starts, pass pca_components directly. Non-monotone features harder. many features non-monotone relationships latent ordering, results become initialisation-sensitive. Partitioning easier trajectory recovery. Even inferred orderings imperfect, feature partition can still accurate. Check objective trace. Within one fixed intrinsic_dim, sustained decreases red flag; one-step drop freeze-affected iteration possible excluded convergence accounting. Compare across intrinsic_dim drop_unused_ordering = FALSE. mode stored objective keeps fixed requested-M comparison semantics. drop_unused_ordering = TRUE, becomes post-drop fit objective compared across requested dimensions.

what: Forward greedy feature partition into two orderings (csmoothEM version) — forward_two_ordering_partition_csmooth
title: Forward greedy feature partition into two orderings (csmoothEM version) — forward_two_ordering_partition_csmooth
text: Greedily partitions features (columns X) two groups, associated latent ordering (represented responsibilities \(\Gamma\) K components). algorithm mirrors structure two_ordering_smoothEM_v2: Choose seed feature j1 fit 1D csmoothEM model X[, j1]         obtain \(\Gamma_1\) parameters \(\theta_1\). Choose second seed feature j2 fit 1D csmoothEM model X[, j2]         obtain \(\Gamma_2\) parameters \(\theta_2\). unassigned features remain: Score remaining feature \(\Gamma_1\) \(\Gamma_2\). Select feature largest absolute score gap assign better ordering. Append 1D fitted object ordering's parameters update \(\Gamma\) E-step. Optionally run short csmoothEM refinement ordering (controlled greedy_em_refine). Seeding: greedy_start_index provided, used j1. Otherwise, seeding="variance" chooses j1 maximum-variance feature. Otherwise, seeding="correlation" chooses j1 feature correlated         (default, absolute correlation) ordering vector seed_ordering_vec.         seed_ordering_vec NULL, ordering vector defaults first PC score X. j2: greedy_start_index2 provided, used j2. Else seeding="correlation" greedy_start_index NULL, choose j2         feature least correlated ordering vector used j1. Else (default), choose j2 feature worst alignment score \(\Gamma_1\). Scoring: score_mode="none" uses plug-(ELBO/Q-like) score fixed \(\lambda=1\). score_mode="ml" uses collapsed (marginal-like) score \(\lambda\) optimized per feature.

text: Let \(Q_K\) denote RW(q) precision along components. separable prior \(Q_{\mathrm{full}} = I_d \otimes Q_K\), rank deficiency \(rw_q\) per coordinate, convenient degrees--freedom term \(r = K - rw_q\). adaptive = "prior" (obsolete), initializer estimates coordinate penalty : $$\lambda_j = r \big/ \left( \mu_{j\cdot}^\top Q_{j,\mathrm{base}} \mu_{j\cdot} \right),$$ \(\mu_{j\cdot}\) length-K vector component means coordinate \(j\), \(Q_{j,\mathrm{base}}\) coordinate-specific base precision returned .compute_Qbase_j() (equivalently, \(Q_K\) possibly rescaled relative_lambda = TRUE). estimate clamped \([\text{lambda_min},\text{lambda_max}]\). mode heuristic treated convergence-guaranteed update. adaptive = "ml", warm start uses collapsed-ML routine. mode, do_csmoothEM() dispatches do_csmoothEM_ml_collapsed(), optimizes collapsed (Laplace-exact, Gaussian) objective records ml_trace collapsed objective \(\mathcal{C}\). relative_lambda = TRUE, base precision \(Q_{j,\mathrm{base}}\) scaled coordinate variance proxy: modelName = "homoskedastic": scale 1 / sigma2[j]. modelName = "heteroskedastic": scale 1 / sum_k pi_k sigma2[j,k].

what: Score a single feature given responsibilities (Gamma) — score_feature_given_Gamma
title: Score a single feature given responsibilities (Gamma) — score_feature_given_Gamma
text: Computes alignment score single feature \(x_j\) fixed responsibilities matrix \(\Gamma \\mathbb{R}^{n\times K}\). score used greedy feature partitioning multiple latent orderings. Two score modes supported: score_mode = "none" Plug-(ELBO/Q-like) score. smoothing parameter \(\lambda\) fixed     lambda_init (default 1). score depends MAP component means     \(\hat\mu_{j\cdot}\) via weighted SSE. score_mode = "ml" Collapsed (marginal-like) score: plug-score plus Laplace curvature correction     \(+\frac{K}{2}\log(2\pi) - \frac12\log|A_j|\). mode, \(\lambda\) optimized     1D maximization \(\log \lambda \[\log(\text{lambda_min}),\log(\text{lambda_max})]\). fitted 1D quantities \(\hat\mu_{j\cdot}\) \(\hat\sigma_j^2\) returned can appended partition's csmooth-style parameters greedy growth.

title: Score a single feature given responsibilities (Gamma) — score_feature_given_Gamma

title: Score a single feature given responsibilities (Gamma) — score_feature_given_Gamma
text: xj Numeric vector length n (one feature). Gamma Numeric matrix (n x K) responsibilities. Q_K Numeric matrix (K x K); base RW precision (lambda=1). rw_q Integer \(\ge 0\). Rank deficiency along K (RW order). score_mode One "ml" "none". relative_lambda Logical; TRUE use \(Q_{base} = Q_K / \sigma_j^2\) (homoskedastic scaling). lambda_min, lambda_max Positive bounds \(\lambda\) score_mode="ml". nugget Nonnegative scalar added \(\sigma_j^2\). optimize_lambda Logical NULL. NULL, defaults TRUE "ml" FALSE "none". lambda_init Positive scalar. Fixed \(\lambda\) score_mode="none" (default 1), initial value optimize_lambda=FALSE. max_sigma_iter Integer \(\ge 1\). Number (lambda -> mu -> sigma2) refresh steps.

title: Score a single feature given responsibilities (Gamma) — score_feature_given_Gamma

what: Soft dual-trajectory partition via annealed CAVI — soft_two_trajectory_EM
title: Soft dual-trajectory partition via annealed CAVI — soft_two_trajectory_EM
text: Jointly infers two latent 1-D orderings soft feature-level partition via coordinate-ascent variational inference (CAVI). algorithm alternates : Outer E-step: score feature current Gamma_1, Gamma_2 via   compute_C_by_coord_csmooth(); compute Softmax(S/T) get   soft feature weights w_jm. Inner EM: refit trajectory via do_csmoothEM_weighted().   E-step uses weighted log-likelihood contributions,   adaptive = "ml" collapsed lambda/sigma2   hyper-step reweighted well. final MAP refresh mu   unchanged. Phase 1 anneals temperature T T_start T_end escape local optima. outer step performs inner_iter weighted trajectory updates recomputing feature weights. Phase 2 runs untempered updates T = T_end recorded objective stabilises. legacy csmooth_em-based soft partition routine. retained benchmark comparison internal regression testing. new analyses, prefer soft_two_trajectory_cavi, uses explicit variational cavi model throughout recommended dual-ordering soft-partition backend.
